title: Paint House III
hints: Think about processing houses one by one. What state do you need to track to decide the cost of the next house while counting neighborhoods?
Use dynamic programming where the state includes the current house index, the number of neighborhoods formed so far, and the color of the previous house.
For each unpainted house, iterate over all possible colors. If the color matches the previous house, the neighborhood count stays the same; otherwise, it increases by one.
